title: process_mail_from assumes an internal EventMachine @state array shape
body: MailCatcher::Smtp#process_mail_from does `@state -= [:mail_from, :rcpt, :data]` to reset state for a second MAIL FROM per RFC 2821, relying on EventMachine::Protocols::SmtpServer's private @state ivar being an Array. A newer eventmachine release changing that representation would silently break multi-MAIL-FROM handling.

title: Only the SIZE= extension is stripped from MAIL FROM
body: receive_sender strips a trailing ` SIZE=\d+` from the sender address but other common SMTP extensions like `BODY=8BITMIME` or `SMTPUTF8` are left attached to the sender string, which then shows up mangled in the caught-message list.

title: Thin websocket-close patch can raise on abrupt shutdown
body: The monkeypatch on Thin::Backends::Base#stop calls `connection.socket_stream.close_connection_after_writing` for every value in @connections, but during an abrupt Ctrl+C shutdown some entries' socket_stream can already be nil, raising NoMethodError instead of just skipping that connection.

title: Document the message purge threshold
body: receive_message calls MailCatcher::Mail.delete_older_messages! after every received email, but nothing in the README or a comment explains the retention window or count used to decide which messages get purged. Could this be documented, ideally with the relevant config option if one exists?
